args.task: Perform comprehensive subdomain enumeration for scaa.us. Use passive and active techniques (subfinder + supplemental bruteforce), enumerate DNS records (A/AAAA/CNAME), detect wildcard DNS, and check for potential subdomain takeover candidates. Produce a deduplicated list of discovered subdomains/IPs, note TTLs and authoritative nameservers, and save findings to a shared note. Report high-value hosts for follow-up port/service scanning.

args.task: Perform active port and service scanning for scaa.us and discovered subdomains. Use naabu for fast port discovery and nmap for service/version/os detection on identified IPs. Produce a deduplicated host:port list with service names, versions, and any exposed management panels or databases. Highlight high-value services (SSH, RDP, database ports, web admin interfaces) and save results to a shared note for follow-up web testing.

args.task:
Perform deep web crawling and endpoint discovery for http://scaa.us and any discovered subdomains. Use katana for JS-aware crawling (discover routes rendered client-side) and ffuf for targeted directory/parameter fuzzing. Actions:
  - Crawl all reachable pages (unauthenticated by default) and capture URLs, HTTP methods, parameters, forms, cookies, and response codes
  - Extract and analyze JavaScript bundles to enumerate AJAX/API endpoints and dynamic routes
  - Probe for OpenAPI/Swagger, sitemap.xml, robots.txt, and common admin paths
  - Use ffuf to fuzz common directories and parameter names on high-value hosts
  - Identify file upload endpoints, authentication pages, search fields, and any input points that accept user data
  - Produce a deduplicated sitemap and a prioritized list of input points (with method, parameter names, and sample request) suitable for follow-up vulnerability testing
  - Save findings to a shared note and mark top-priority endpoints for the vulnerability-specific agents (XSS/SQLi)
args.name: Crawler & Endpoint Mapper

result.message: Agent 'Crawler & Endpoint Mapper' created and started asynchronously

args.command:
echo "Resolving scaa.us (A/AAAA/NS)..."
dig +short A scaa.us || true
dig +short AAAA scaa.us || true
dig +short NS scaa.us || true

echo
echo "Running naabu (fast port discovery, top-ports 100) against scaa.us..."
naabu -host scaa.us -top-ports 100 -scan-type c -Pn -rate 150 -c 25 -timeout 1000 -retries 1 -verify -silent -oL naabu_scaa.txt || true

echo
echo "naabu results (raw):"
if [ -f naabu_scaa.txt ]; then
  cat naabu_scaa.txt
else
  echo "(no naabu output file)"
fi

echo
echo "Running nmap top-ports 100 with service detection (quick enrichment)..."
nmap -n -Pn --top-ports 100 -sV -T4 --max-retries 1 -oN nmap_scaa_top.txt scaa.us || true

echo
echo "nmap (top ports) snippet:"
if [ -f nmap_scaa_top.txt ]; then
  sed -n '1,200p' nmap_scaa_top.txt
else
  echo "(no nmap_scaa_top.txt)"
fi

echo
echo "Extracting ip:port pairs from naabu output..."
grep -Eo '([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}:[0-9]+' naabu_scaa.txt | sort -u > hosts_ports.txt || true
if [ ! -s hosts_ports.txt ]; then
  echo "No ip:port lines from naabu; attempting to extract from nmap output..."
  # Try to find lines like "Nmap scan report for <ip>" then ports
  awk '/Nmap scan report/{host=$NF} /^[0-9]+\/tcp/ {print host ":" gensub(/\/tcp.*/,"","g",$1)}' nmap_scaa_top.txt | sort -u > hosts_ports.txt || true
fi

echo
echo "hosts_ports.txt contents (deduplicated):"
if [ -f hosts_ports.txt ]; then
  cat hosts_ports.txt || true
else
  echo "(hosts_ports.txt not present)"
fi

echo
echo "Running targeted nmap enrichment (per-IP service/version) for discovered ip:port entries..."
if [ -s hosts_ports.txt ]; then
  awk -F: '{print $1}' hosts_ports.txt | sort -u | while read ip; do
    ports=$(grep "^$ip:" hosts_ports.txt | awk -F: '{print $2}' | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
    if [ -n "$ports" ]; then
      echo
      echo "==== nmap enrichment for $ip ports: $ports ===="
      nmap -n -Pn -sV -p $ports -T4 --max-retries 1 -oN nmap_${ip}.txt $ip || true
      sed -n '1,200p' nmap_${ip}.txt || true
    fi
  done
else
  echo "No ip:port pairs discovered; running quick nmap against resolved A records on common ports (22,80,443,8080,8443)..."
  IPS=$(dig +short A scaa.us | tr '\n' ' ')
  for ip in $IPS; do
    echo
    echo "==== nmap quick for $ip ports: 22,80,443,8080,8443 ===="
    nmap -n -Pn -sV -p 22,80,443,8080,8443 -T4 --max-retries 1 -oN nmap_${ip}.txt $ip || true
    sed -n '1,200p' nmap_${ip}.txt || true
  done
fi

echo
echo "Compiling compiled_services.txt (host:port service version summary)..."
echo "Host:Port Service Version (source: nmap)" > compiled_services.txt
for f in nmap_*.txt; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    host=$(awk '/Nmap scan report for/ {print $NF}' $f | head -n1)
    awk -v h="$host" '/^[0-9]+\/tcp/ { printf("%s %s %s %s\n", h, $1, $3, $4) }' "$f" >> compiled_services.txt || true
  fi
done

echo
echo "compiled_services.txt:"
if [ -f compiled_services.txt ]; then
  cat compiled_services.txt || true
else
  echo "(no compiled_services.txt)"
fi

echo
echo "Scan completed."

args.task:
Perform authorization and IDOR testing across all discovered endpoints for scaa.us. Use endpoint mappings from the Crawler & Endpoint Mapper and captured proxy traffic. Actions:
  - Enumerate object identifiers (IDs, resource keys) across endpoints and parameters
  - Attempt horizontal and vertical IDOR checks by manipulating identifiers and roles
  - Test function-level authorization by calling privileged endpoints as unprivileged users and via direct API calls
  - Test JWT/session handling for privilege escalation and algorithm/claim manipulation
  - Automate parameter fuzzing for access-control bypasses and record anomalies
  - Triage findings by confidence and impact; for every high-confidence issue, spawn a dedicated validation agent to produce PoC and reporting artifacts
  - Save a prioritized list of suspected and confirmed authorization issues (with sample requests, response differences, and suggested next steps) to a shared note for the root agent
args.name: Authorization & IDOR Tester

args.task:
Perform automated vulnerability discovery across all endpoints discovered by the Crawler & Endpoint Mapper for scaa.us and any discovered subdomains.
Focus on SQL injection, cross-site scripting (reflected, stored, DOM-based), and server-side request forgery (SSRF).
Actions:
  - Enumerate parameters and potential injection points using arjun and proxy-captured traffic.
  - Fuzz directories and parameter names with ffuf to find hidden endpoints and injectable parameters.
  - Run targeted sqlmap scans (safe, scoped to identified parameters) to detect boolean/time/UNION-based SQLi and capture DB fingerprinting evidence.
  - Run XSS discovery using automated payload sets, reflection checks, and DOM analysis of JS bundles to detect reflected/stored/DOM XSS.
  - Test SSRF candidates on URL-fetching endpoints with controlled probes (loopback, private ranges, and link-local addresses) and timing/response analysis to infer server-side fetch behavior.
  - Use nuclei templates for quick triage of common issues and correlate findings with crawler output.
  - Rate-limit and scope scans to avoid service disruption; record request/response samples, confidence levels (low/medium/high), and remediation hints.
Deliverables:
  - Deduplicated list of candidate vulnerabilities with sample requests/responses and confidence.
  - Prioritized endpoints recommended for dedicated Validation Agents (one per confirmed/high-confidence finding).
  - Save results to a shared note for the root agent and other subagents to consume.
args.name: Vulnerability Discovery Agent
